create.help: Create a batch payout to one or more recipients. For a single recipient, use --receiver and --amount. For multiple recipients, use --items-json with a JSON array.

options.name: --email-subject
options.help: Subject line for PayPal notification email

options.name: --items-json
options.help: JSON array for multi-recipient batch payouts

examples: paypal payouts create --receiver user@example.com --amount 25.50 --note "Payment for services"
create.usage_instructions: Create a PayPal batch payout. For one recipient pass --receiver and --amount; for multiple recipients pass --items-json.

get.help: Get batch payout status and details.
get.usage_instructions: Fetch a PayPal payout batch by batch ID. Add --table for a concise status table.

get-item.usage_instructions: Fetch an individual PayPal payout item by item ID. Add --table for a concise item status table.

cancel-item.help: Cancel an unclaimed payout item. Only items with UNCLAIMED status can be cancelled.
cancel-item.usage_instructions: Cancel an unclaimed PayPal payout item by item ID. Only unclaimed items can be cancelled.

payouts.usage_instructions: Use paypal payouts for PayPal batch payout creation, status lookup, payout-item lookup, and unclaimed item cancellation.

list.help: List transactions from the PayPal Transaction Search API.

options.name: --start-date
options.help: Start of the transaction range. YYYY-MM-DD becomes 00:00:00Z. Naive datetimes are interpreted as UTC

options.name: --end-date
options.help: End of the transaction range. YYYY-MM-DD becomes 23:59:59Z. Naive datetimes are interpreted as UTC

options.name: --limit
options.help: RANGE [x>=1]       Maximum number of transactions to return

options.name: --filter
options.help: Client-side filter: field:op:value. Supports nested fields such as transaction_info.transaction_status:eq:S

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include in output

options.name: --page-size
options.help: RANGE [1<=x<=500]  Number of records to request per PayPal page (max 500)

options.name: --all-records
options.help: Include non-balance-affecting records by sending balance_affecting_records_only=N

list.usage_instructions: List business-account transactions by --start-date and --end-date. Date-only values are normalized to full UTC days, ranges longer than 31 days are chunked automatically, JSON output is an array, and --table, --limit, --filter, --properties, --page-size, and --all-records are supported.

get.help: Get transaction-search matches for a PayPal transaction ID.

options.name: --start-date
options.help: Start of the search range. YYYY-MM-DD becomes 00:00:00Z. Naive datetimes are interpreted as UTC

options.name: --end-date
options.help: End of the search range. YYYY-MM-DD becomes 23:59:59Z. Naive datetimes are interpreted as UTC

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of transaction detail fields to include in output

options.name: --page-size
options.help: RANGE [1<=x<=500]  Number of records to request per PayPal page (max 500)

options.name: --all-records
options.help: Include non-balance-affecting records by sending balance_affecting_records_only=N

get.usage_instructions: Search for PayPal transaction ID matches within --start-date and --end-date. PayPal transaction IDs can have multiple matching records, so JSON output is an object with transaction_details and matching_transaction_count. Add --table or --properties for narrower output.

transactions.usage_instructions: Use paypal transactions for PayPal Transaction Search API reads against a business account.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

login.usage_instructions: Store PayPal OAuth client credentials for a profile. Use --force to replace existing credentials.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

logout.usage_instructions: Clear stored PayPal credentials for the selected profile.

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test``.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

status.usage_instructions: Check PayPal authentication status using the shared profiles JSON shape. Read profiles[].authenticated and credential_types.oauth for details.

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Run live credential checks for PayPal auth profiles. Use --verbose for detailed checks.

list.help: List all profiles and show their auth types and active state.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: List PayPal auth profiles, including auth type and active state. Supports --table, --limit, --filter, and --properties.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

get.usage_instructions: Show details for a named PayPal auth profile. Add --table for table output.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Create a new PayPal auth profile from the profile template.

select.help: Activate a profile within its auth type.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to activate within its auth type

select.usage_instructions: Use auth profiles select NAME to activate NAME within its auth type. Other auth types keep their active profiles unchanged.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a named PayPal auth profile and its data. Use --force to skip confirmation.

remove.help: Delete a profile and its data.
remove.usage_instructions: Delete a named PayPal auth profile and its data. Use --force to skip confirmation.

profiles.usage_instructions: Use paypal auth profiles to list, inspect, create, select, or remove PayPal auth profiles.

auth.usage_instructions: Use paypal auth to configure OAuth client credentials, verify profiles, and manage authentication profiles.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Clear the PayPal CLI response cache.

cache.usage_instructions: Use paypal cache for local response-cache maintenance.

usage_instructions: Use the paypal CLI for PayPal business-account API operations. Run auth status/login before live API calls, use payouts for batch payout operations, and use transactions list/get for Transaction Search API reads.
